output: copy C:\Windows\System32\sc.exe C:\Windows\Temp\not_sc.exe && C:\Windows\Temp\not_sc.exe create MyService binPath= "C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe"
explanation: The rule relies on the 'Image' field ending with '\sc.exe'. By copying the legitimate sc.exe binary to a different name (e.g., not_sc.exe) and executing it from there, the logic 'Image|endswith' is bypassed while the functionality remains the same.

The rule specifically monitors process creation for 'sc.exe'. Using PowerShell's 'New-Service' cmdlet performs the same action via API calls rather than the sc.exe utility, completely avoiding the 'selection' criteria.

The rule is case-sensitive regarding the 'CommandLine' filter in many SIEM implementations. While the rule looks for 'binPath', the Windows SC utility accepts 'binpath' (lowercase). Depending on the regex/string match sensitivity of the engine, this variation may bypass the 'contains|all' requirement.
